Assignment1_AML

Manpreet Kaur

2026-02-16

Step 1: Install & Load Libraries

#install.packages("keras3")
library(keras3)

Step 2: Load and Preprocess Data

# Load IMDB dataset
max_features <- 10000   # keep top 10k words
maxlen <- 500           # cut texts after 500 words

imdb <- dataset_imdb(num_words = max_features)
c(c(x_train, y_train), c(x_test, y_test)) %<-% imdb

# Pad sequences so all reviews are the same length
x_train <- pad_sequences(x_train, maxlen = maxlen)
x_test  <- pad_sequences(x_test,  maxlen = maxlen)

Step 3: Baseline Model (2 hidden layers, relu, 16 units)

baseline_model <- keras_model_sequential() %>%
  layer_embedding(input_dim = max_features, output_dim = 32, input_length = maxlen) %>%
  layer_flatten() %>%
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "relu") %>%
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "relu") %>%
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "sigmoid")

baseline_model %>% compile(
  optimizer = "rmsprop",
  loss = "binary_crossentropy",
  metrics = c("accuracy")
)

history_baseline <- baseline_model %>% fit(
  x_train, y_train,
  epochs = 20,
  batch_size = 512,
  validation_split = 0.2
)
## Epoch 1/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 35ms/step - accuracy: 0.5094 - loss: 0.6930 - val_accuracy: 0.5174 - val_loss: 0.6925
## Epoch 2/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.5713 - loss: 0.6840 - val_accuracy: 0.6268 - val_loss: 0.6610
## Epoch 3/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.6165 - loss: 0.6489 - val_accuracy: 0.7128 - val_loss: 0.5986
## Epoch 4/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.7186 - loss: 0.5548 - val_accuracy: 0.7326 - val_loss: 0.5224
## Epoch 5/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 24ms/step - accuracy: 0.7836 - loss: 0.4523 - val_accuracy: 0.6890 - val_loss: 0.6185
## Epoch 6/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.8328 - loss: 0.3758 - val_accuracy: 0.7684 - val_loss: 0.4832
## Epoch 7/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 24ms/step - accuracy: 0.8815 - loss: 0.2886 - val_accuracy: 0.8360 - val_loss: 0.3676
## Epoch 8/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 24ms/step - accuracy: 0.9054 - loss: 0.2405 - val_accuracy: 0.8166 - val_loss: 0.4127
## Epoch 9/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 24ms/step - accuracy: 0.9059 - loss: 0.2259 - val_accuracy: 0.8348 - val_loss: 0.3938
## Epoch 10/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 24ms/step - accuracy: 0.9215 - loss: 0.2019 - val_accuracy: 0.8114 - val_loss: 0.4468
## Epoch 11/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9648 - loss: 0.1123 - val_accuracy: 0.8252 - val_loss: 0.4330
## Epoch 12/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9466 - loss: 0.1346 - val_accuracy: 0.8314 - val_loss: 0.4275
## Epoch 13/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 24ms/step - accuracy: 0.9865 - loss: 0.0545 - val_accuracy: 0.8298 - val_loss: 0.4727
## Epoch 14/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9265 - loss: 0.1841 - val_accuracy: 0.8390 - val_loss: 0.4375
## Epoch 15/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 24ms/step - accuracy: 0.9923 - loss: 0.0337 - val_accuracy: 0.8282 - val_loss: 0.5536
## Epoch 16/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 24ms/step - accuracy: 0.9505 - loss: 0.1210 - val_accuracy: 0.8212 - val_loss: 0.4624
## Epoch 17/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9787 - loss: 0.0730 - val_accuracy: 0.8260 - val_loss: 0.5806
## Epoch 18/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9981 - loss: 0.0104 - val_accuracy: 0.8226 - val_loss: 0.6419
## Epoch 19/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9994 - loss: 0.0055 - val_accuracy: 0.8232 - val_loss: 0.7075
## Epoch 20/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9995 - loss: 0.0036 - val_accuracy: 0.8212 - val_loss: 0.7530

Step 4: Variations You Need to Test 1. Vary Number of Hidden Layers

# One hidden layer
model_1layer <- keras_model_sequential() %>%
  layer_embedding(max_features, 32, input_length = maxlen) %>%
  layer_flatten() %>%
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "relu") %>%
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "sigmoid")
# Three hidden layers
model_3layer <- keras_model_sequential() %>%
  layer_embedding(max_features, 32, input_length = maxlen) %>%
  layer_flatten() %>%
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "relu") %>%
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "relu") %>%
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "relu") %>%
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "sigmoid")
  1. Vary Units (32, 64, etc.)
model_units <- keras_model_sequential() %>%
  layer_embedding(max_features, 32, input_length = maxlen) %>%
  layer_flatten() %>%
  layer_dense(units = 64, activation = "relu") %>%   # Try 32, 64, 128
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "sigmoid")
  1. Change Loss Function to MSE
model_mse <- keras_model_sequential() %>%
  layer_embedding(max_features, 32, input_length = maxlen) %>%
  layer_flatten() %>%
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "relu") %>%
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "sigmoid")

model_mse %>% compile(
  optimizer = "rmsprop",
  loss = "mse",   # Mean Squared Error
  metrics = c("accuracy")
)
  1. Change Activation to Tanh
model_tanh <- keras_model_sequential() %>%
  layer_embedding(max_features, 32, input_length = maxlen) %>%
  layer_flatten() %>%
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "tanh") %>%   # tanh
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "tanh") %>%
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "sigmoid")
  1. Add Dropout / Regularization
model_dropout <- keras_model_sequential() %>%
  layer_embedding(max_features, 32, input_length = maxlen) %>%
  layer_flatten() %>%
  layer_dense(units = 64, activation = "relu", kernel_regularizer = regularizer_l2(0.001)) %>%
  layer_dropout(rate = 0.5) %>%   # Dropout layer
  layer_dense(units = 64, activation = "relu") %>%
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "sigmoid")

Step 5: Compare Results

For each model: Example with baseline model

history_baseline <- baseline_model %>% fit(
  x_train, y_train,
  epochs = 20,
  batch_size = 512,
  validation_split = 0.2
)
## Epoch 1/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9993 - loss: 0.0028 - val_accuracy: 0.8164 - val_loss: 0.8312
## Epoch 2/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9999 - loss: 9.4671e-04 - val_accuracy: 0.8190 - val_loss: 0.8963
## Epoch 3/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 24ms/step - accuracy: 0.9772 - loss: 0.0790 - val_accuracy: 0.8274 - val_loss: 0.6452
## Epoch 4/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9997 - loss: 0.0051 - val_accuracy: 0.8216 - val_loss: 0.9040
## Epoch 5/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9993 - loss: 0.0027 - val_accuracy: 0.8184 - val_loss: 0.9309
## Epoch 6/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 3.4025e-04 - val_accuracy: 0.8198 - val_loss: 0.9935
## Epoch 7/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 2.0832e-04 - val_accuracy: 0.8202 - val_loss: 1.0356
## Epoch 8/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 1.7103e-04 - val_accuracy: 0.8186 - val_loss: 1.0617
## Epoch 9/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9999 - loss: 1.8312e-04 - val_accuracy: 0.8166 - val_loss: 1.0847
## Epoch 10/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 1.0976e-04 - val_accuracy: 0.8182 - val_loss: 1.1107
## Epoch 11/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 9.5135e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8192 - val_loss: 1.1280
## Epoch 12/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 8.5403e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8192 - val_loss: 1.1463
## Epoch 13/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 8.0050e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8188 - val_loss: 1.1565
## Epoch 14/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 6.9689e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8190 - val_loss: 1.1737
## Epoch 15/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 6.6617e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8180 - val_loss: 1.1766
## Epoch 16/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 5.9741e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8180 - val_loss: 1.1912
## Epoch 17/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 5.5651e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8200 - val_loss: 1.2008
## Epoch 18/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 5.2360e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8194 - val_loss: 1.2110
## Epoch 19/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 4.9362e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8192 - val_loss: 1.2163
## Epoch 20/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 1.0000 - loss: 4.6995e-05 - val_accuracy: 0.8180 - val_loss: 1.2255
score_baseline <- baseline_model %>% evaluate(x_test, y_test)
## 782/782 - 0s - 481us/step - accuracy: 0.8215 - loss: 1.1513
print(score_baseline)
## $accuracy
## [1] 0.82148
## 
## $loss
## [1] 1.151261

Example with 1 hidden layer model

model_1layer %>% compile(
  optimizer = "rmsprop",
  loss = "binary_crossentropy",
  metrics = c("accuracy")
)

history_1layer <- model_1layer %>% fit(
  x_train, y_train,
  epochs = 20,
  batch_size = 512,
  validation_split = 0.2
)
## Epoch 1/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 33ms/step - accuracy: 0.5323 - loss: 0.6905 - val_accuracy: 0.5242 - val_loss: 0.6824
## Epoch 2/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.6320 - loss: 0.6552 - val_accuracy: 0.5678 - val_loss: 0.6788

## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9186 - loss: 0.2119 - val_accuracy: 0.8318 - val_loss: 0.3745
## Epoch 10/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9352 - loss: 0.1836 - val_accuracy: 0.8280 - val_loss: 0.4064

## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9377 - loss: 0.1697 - val_accuracy: 0.8314 - val_loss: 0.3824
## Epoch 12/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9599 - loss: 0.1243 - val_accuracy: 0.8294 - val_loss: 0.4062
## Epoch 13/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9718 - loss: 0.0999 - val_accuracy: 0.8384 - val_loss: 0.3850
## Epoch 14/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9844 - loss: 0.0723 - val_accuracy: 0.8314 - val_loss: 0.4316
## Epoch 15/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9783 - loss: 0.0766 - val_accuracy: 0.8330 - val_loss: 0.4291

## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9832 - loss: 0.0594 - val_accuracy: 0.8390 - val_loss: 0.4376

## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9832 - loss: 0.0570 - val_accuracy: 0.8190 - val_loss: 0.4950

## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9971 - loss: 0.0266 - val_accuracy: 0.8272 - val_loss: 0.4925
## Epoch 19/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9886 - loss: 0.0399 - val_accuracy: 0.8236 - val_loss: 0.5169
## Epoch 20/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9962 - loss: 0.0251 - val_accuracy: 0.8282 - val_loss: 0.5190
score_1layer <- model_1layer %>% evaluate(x_test, y_test)
## 782/782 - 0s - 482us/step - accuracy: 0.8320 - loss: 0.5014
print(score_1layer)
## $accuracy
## [1] 0.832
## 
## $loss
## [1] 0.5014032

Example with 3 Hidden Layers Model

# Compile
model_3layer %>% compile(
  optimizer = "rmsprop",
  loss = "binary_crossentropy",
  metrics = c("accuracy")
)

# Fit
history_3layer <- model_3layer %>% fit(
  x_train, y_train,
  epochs = 20,
  batch_size = 512,
  validation_split = 0.2
)
## Epoch 1/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 35ms/step - accuracy: 0.5010 - loss: 0.6933 - val_accuracy: 0.4926 - val_loss: 0.6932
## Epoch 2/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.5301 - loss: 0.6921 - val_accuracy: 0.5074 - val_loss: 0.6928
## Epoch 3/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.5982 - loss: 0.6787 - val_accuracy: 0.5622 - val_loss: 0.6778

## Epoch 10/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9054 - loss: 0.2413 - val_accuracy: 0.7784 - val_loss: 0.5420

## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9280 - loss: 0.1875 - val_accuracy: 0.8414 - val_loss: 0.3925

## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9456 - loss: 0.1485 - val_accuracy: 0.8376 - val_loss: 0.4149
## Epoch 13/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9752 - loss: 0.0847 - val_accuracy: 0.8316 - val_loss: 0.4593
## Epoch 14/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9833 - loss: 0.0615 - val_accuracy: 0.8316 - val_loss: 0.5229
## Epoch 15/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9381 - loss: 0.1564 - val_accuracy: 0.8264 - val_loss: 0.4354
## Epoch 16/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9825 - loss: 0.0557 - val_accuracy: 0.8292 - val_loss: 0.5795
## Epoch 17/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9973 - loss: 0.0148 - val_accuracy: 0.8260 - val_loss: 0.6626

## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9987 - loss: 0.0073 - val_accuracy: 0.8270 - val_loss: 0.7846

## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9993 - loss: 0.0033 - val_accuracy: 0.8244 - val_loss: 0.8913
## Epoch 20/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9996 - loss: 0.0019 - val_accuracy: 0.8248 - val_loss: 0.9921
# Evaluate
score_3layer <- model_3layer %>% evaluate(x_test, y_test)
## 782/782 - 0s - 488us/step - accuracy: 0.8244 - loss: 0.9634
print(score_3layer)
## $accuracy
## [1] 0.82436
## 
## $loss
## [1] 0.9634125

Example with More Units (64)

# Compile
model_units %>% compile(
  optimizer = "rmsprop",
  loss = "binary_crossentropy",
  metrics = c("accuracy")
)

# Fit
history_units <- model_units %>% fit(
  x_train, y_train,
  epochs = 20,
  batch_size = 512,
  validation_split = 0.2
)
## Epoch 1/20
## 40/40 - 2s - 39ms/step - accuracy: 0.5183 - loss: 0.6922 - val_accuracy: 0.5252 - val_loss: 0.6906

## 40/40 - 1s - 32ms/step - accuracy: 0.9202 - loss: 0.1935 - val_accuracy: 0.8364 - val_loss: 0.4065

## 40/40 - 1s - 32ms/step - accuracy: 0.9689 - loss: 0.0998 - val_accuracy: 0.8258 - val_loss: 0.4724

## 40/40 - 1s - 31ms/step - accuracy: 0.9768 - loss: 0.0818 - val_accuracy: 0.8370 - val_loss: 0.4465

## 40/40 - 1s - 31ms/step - accuracy: 0.9373 - loss: 0.2900 - val_accuracy: 0.8160 - val_loss: 0.4579
## Epoch 20/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 32ms/step - accuracy: 0.9747 - loss: 0.0817 - val_accuracy: 0.8282 - val_loss: 0.5467
# Evaluate
score_units <- model_units %>% evaluate(x_test, y_test)
## 782/782 - 0s - 607us/step - accuracy: 0.8345 - loss: 0.5193
print(score_units)
## $accuracy
## [1] 0.83452
## 
## $loss
## [1] 0.519304

Example: MSE Loss Function

# Compile
model_mse %>% compile(
  optimizer = "rmsprop",
  loss = "mse",   # Using Mean Squared Error
  metrics = c("accuracy")
)

# Fit
history_mse <- model_mse %>% fit(
  x_train, y_train,
  epochs = 20,
  batch_size = 512,
  validation_split = 0.2
)
## Epoch 1/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 32ms/step - accuracy: 0.5039 - loss: 0.2499 - val_accuracy: 0.5024 - val_loss: 0.2500
## Epoch 2/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.5244 - loss: 0.2483 - val_accuracy: 0.5422 - val_loss: 0.2488
## Epoch 3/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.5908 - loss: 0.2402 - val_accuracy: 0.5644 - val_loss: 0.2374
## Epoch 4/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.7006 - loss: 0.2068 - val_accuracy: 0.6722 - val_loss: 0.2035
## Epoch 5/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.7594 - loss: 0.1683 - val_accuracy: 0.7336 - val_loss: 0.1771
## Epoch 6/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.7856 - loss: 0.1520 - val_accuracy: 0.7178 - val_loss: 0.1904
## Epoch 7/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.8298 - loss: 0.1229 - val_accuracy: 0.7938 - val_loss: 0.1435
## Epoch 8/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.8554 - loss: 0.1063 - val_accuracy: 0.7658 - val_loss: 0.1610
## Epoch 9/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.8702 - loss: 0.0977 - val_accuracy: 0.8026 - val_loss: 0.1368
## Epoch 10/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.8921 - loss: 0.0851 - val_accuracy: 0.7898 - val_loss: 0.1424
## Epoch 11/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9111 - loss: 0.0729 - val_accuracy: 0.8204 - val_loss: 0.1258
## Epoch 12/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9201 - loss: 0.0669 - val_accuracy: 0.8360 - val_loss: 0.1172
## Epoch 13/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9281 - loss: 0.0611 - val_accuracy: 0.8332 - val_loss: 0.1208
## Epoch 14/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9467 - loss: 0.0490 - val_accuracy: 0.8270 - val_loss: 0.1222
## Epoch 15/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9477 - loss: 0.0465 - val_accuracy: 0.8108 - val_loss: 0.1350
## Epoch 16/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9584 - loss: 0.0397 - val_accuracy: 0.8284 - val_loss: 0.1221
## Epoch 17/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9596 - loss: 0.0382 - val_accuracy: 0.8248 - val_loss: 0.1241
## Epoch 18/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9761 - loss: 0.0264 - val_accuracy: 0.8126 - val_loss: 0.1328
## Epoch 19/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9753 - loss: 0.0274 - val_accuracy: 0.8154 - val_loss: 0.1325
## Epoch 20/20
## 40/40 - 1s - 25ms/step - accuracy: 0.9806 - loss: 0.0221 - val_accuracy: 0.8178 - val_loss: 0.1282
# Evaluate
score_mse <- model_mse %>% evaluate(x_test, y_test)
## 782/782 - 0s - 447us/step - accuracy: 0.8185 - loss: 0.1275
print(score_mse)
## $accuracy
## [1] 0.81852
## 
## $loss
## [1] 0.1274962

Example with Tanh Activation

# Compile
model_tanh %>% compile(
  optimizer = "rmsprop",
  loss = "binary_crossentropy",
  metrics = c("accuracy")
)

# Fit
history_tanh <- model_tanh %>% fit(
  x_train, y_train,
  epochs = 20,
  batch_size = 512,
  validation_split = 0.2
)

Example with Dropout model

model_dropout %>% compile(
  optimizer = "rmsprop",
  loss = "binary_crossentropy",
  metrics = c("accuracy")
)

history_dropout <- model_dropout %>% fit(
  x_train, y_train,
  epochs = 20,
  batch_size = 512,
  validation_split = 0.2
)

score_dropout <- model_dropout %>% evaluate(x_test, y_test)
## 782/782 - 1s - 802us/step - accuracy: 0.8386 - loss: 0.6662
print(score_dropout)
## $accuracy
## [1] 0.83864
## 
## $loss
## [1] 0.6661954

Plot

  1. Lightweight base-R plot (robust to acc / accuracy)
plot_history <- function(history, metric = c("accuracy", "loss")) {
  metric <- match.arg(metric)
  metrics <- history$metrics
  
  # detect accuracy name
  acc_name <- if (!is.null(metrics$accuracy)) "accuracy" else if (!is.null(metrics$acc)) "acc" else NULL
  if (metric == "accuracy" && is.null(acc_name)) stop("No accuracy metric found in history.")
  
  if (metric == "accuracy") {
    train <- metrics[[acc_name]]
    val   <- metrics[[paste0("val_", acc_name)]]
    ylab <- "Accuracy"
  } else {
    train <- metrics$loss
    val   <- metrics$val_loss
    ylab <- "Loss"
  }
  
  epochs <- seq_along(train)
  ylim <- range(c(train, val), na.rm = TRUE)
  
  plot(epochs, train, type = "o", pch = 16, ylim = ylim,
       xlab = "Epoch", ylab = ylab, main = paste("Training vs Validation", ylab))
  lines(epochs, val, type = "o", pch = 17)
  legend("bottomright", legend = c("train", "validation"), pch = c(16,17), lty = 1)
}

Usage:

plot_history(history_baseline, "accuracy")

plot_history(history_baseline, "loss")

  1. Pretty ggplot2 plot (single history)
# requires packages
if(!requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("ggplot2")
if(!requireNamespace("tidyr", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("tidyr")
library(ggplot2); library(tidyr)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
plot_history_gg <- function(history, metric = c("accuracy", "loss")) {
  metric <- match.arg(metric)
  metrics <- history$metrics
  acc_name <- if (!is.null(metrics$accuracy)) "accuracy" else if (!is.null(metrics$acc)) "acc" else NULL
  if (metric == "accuracy" && is.null(acc_name)) stop("No accuracy metric found.")
  
  if (metric == "accuracy") {
    train <- metrics[[acc_name]]
    val   <- metrics[[paste0("val_", acc_name)]]
    ylab <- "Accuracy"
  } else {
    train <- metrics$loss
    val   <- metrics$val_loss
    ylab <- "Loss"
  }
  
  df <- data.frame(
    epoch = 1:length(train),
    train = train,
    validation = val
  )
  df_long <- pivot_longer(df, cols = c("train","validation"), names_to = "set", values_to = "value")
  
  ggplot(df_long, aes(x = epoch, y = value, color = set)) +
    geom_line() + geom_point(size = 1) +
    labs(title = paste("Training vs Validation", ylab), y = ylab, x = "Epoch") +
    theme_minimal()
}

Usage:

plot_history_gg(history_baseline, "accuracy")

  1. Compare multiple histories (ggplot) — great for experiments
# histories: named list of history objects, e.g. list(baseline = history_baseline, one = history_1layer)
compare_histories <- function(histories, metric = c("accuracy", "loss")) {
  metric <- match.arg(metric)
  library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(ggplot2)
  
  df_all <- lapply(names(histories), function(nm) {
    h <- histories[[nm]]$metrics
    acc_name <- if (!is.null(h$accuracy)) "accuracy" else if (!is.null(h$acc)) "acc" else NULL
    if (metric == "accuracy" && is.null(acc_name)) stop(paste("No accuracy metric for", nm))
    
    if (metric == "accuracy") {
      train <- h[[acc_name]]
      val   <- h[[paste0("val_", acc_name)]]
      ylab <- "Accuracy"
    } else {
      train <- h$loss
      val   <- h$val_loss
      ylab <- "Loss"
    }
    df <- data.frame(epoch = 1:length(train),
                     train = train,
                     validation = val,
                     model = nm)
    pivot_longer(df, cols = c("train","validation"), names_to = "set", values_to = "value")
  })
  
  df_combined <- bind_rows(df_all)
  
  ggplot(df_combined, aes(x = epoch, y = value, color = model, linetype = set)) +
    geom_line() + geom_point(size = 0.8) +
    labs(title = paste("Compare models —", metric), x = "Epoch", y = metric) +
    theme_minimal()
}

Usage:

histories_list <- list(
  baseline = history_baseline,
  one_layer = history_1layer,
  dropout = history_dropout
)
compare_histories(histories_list, "accuracy")
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

Summary Table and Graph

# Load libraries
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)

# Collect results (make sure you trained & evaluated all models!)
results <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Baseline (2 layers, relu, 16 units)",
            "1 hidden layer",
            "3 hidden layers",
            "More units (64)",
            "Loss = MSE",
            "Activation = tanh",
            "Dropout + L2 regularization"),

  Test_Accuracy = c(score_baseline$accuracy,
                  score_1layer$accuracy,
                  score_3layer$accuracy,
                  score_units$accuracy,
                  score_mse$accuracy,
                  score_tanh$accuracy,
                  score_dropout$accuracy)

)

# Summary table
kable(results, caption = "Comparison of Model Variants on IMDB Test Set")
Comparison of Model Variants on IMDB Test Set
Model Test_Accuracy
Baseline (2 layers, relu, 16 units) 0.82148
1 hidden layer 0.83200
3 hidden layers 0.82436
More units (64) 0.83452
Loss = MSE 0.81852
Activation = tanh 0.82376
Dropout + L2 regularization 0.83864 
# Comparison bar chart
ggplot(results, aes(x = reorder(Model, Test_Accuracy), y = Test_Accuracy, fill = Model)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "IMDB Model Comparison (Hyperparameter Tuning)",
       y = "Test Accuracy",
       x = "Model Variant") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
